content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
RISK-ON: low volatility 24.4% with broad participation (breadth 7/8 sectors positive), confirming a strong bull pulse. Market structure favors cyclicals with broad-based leadership.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Banking & Finance is the market leader: AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND; YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND; ISCTR (1W: +7.34% | 1M: +20.94%) ABOVE TREND. Transportation also running strong: TAVHL (1W: +8.00% | 1M: +21.32%) ABOVE TREND; THYAO (1W: +6.17% | 1M: +19.25%) ABOVE TREND.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Technology & Telecom is the clear laggard: KONTR (1W: -8.73% | 1M: -30.92%) BELOW TREND; TTKOM (1W: -1.56% | 1M: +9.83%) CORRECTION. Retail & Food shows relative underperformance within the rally: CCOLA (1W: -2.91% | 1M: +0.38%) CORRECTION; SOKM (1W: -1.99% | 1M: +1.13%) CORRECTION.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND; YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND; AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND; TOASO (1W: +10.91% | 1M: +16.95%) ABOVE TREND; EKGYO (1W: +10.20% | 1M: +19.17%) ABOVE TREND.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
KONTR (1W: -8.73% | 1M: -30.92%) BELOW TREND; TUPRS (1W: -2.95% | 1M: -6.12%) BELOW TREND; CCOLA (1W: -2.91% | 1M: +0.38%) CORRECTION; DOHOL (1W: -2.37% | 1M: -0.65%) BELOW TREND; TKFEN (1W: -2.01% | 1M: +15.03%) CORRECTION.

6. Momentum Stocks?
High 1W momentum names to play with trend: TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND; YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND; AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND; TOASO (1W: +10.91% | 1M: +16.95%) ABOVE TREND; EKGYO (1W: +10.20% | 1M: +19.17%) ABOVE TREND.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking is the clear cyclical leader—AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND; YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND; ISCTR (1W: +7.34% | 1M: +20.94%) ABOVE TREND. Momentum and earnings visibility support overweight exposure, but monitor margins and funding costs.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry and autos are firing: TOASO (1W: +10.91% | 1M: +16.95%) ABOVE TREND; ASELS (1W: +8.42% | 1M: +6.55%) ABOVE TREND; EREGL (1W: +1.68% | 1M: +15.30%) ABOVE TREND. Quality cyclicals offer upside while low-volatility names (ARCLK (1W: +0.58% | 1M: +1.47%) ABOVE TREND) lag.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Mixed but constructive: TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND and TRMET (1W: +8.08% | 1M: +13.52%) ABOVE TREND are outperforming, while TUPRS (1W: -2.95% | 1M: -6.12%) BELOW TREND remains weak. Watch oil and feedstock moves as key catalysts for sector rotation.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Overweight cyclicals—banks and select industrials—given RISK-ON backdrop; selectively trim laggards in Technology & Telecom and single-name profit-take on parabolic winners. Maintain cash buffer for any rate or external risk shocks.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND; AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND; TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND; TAVHL (1W: +8.00% | 1M: +21.32%) ABOVE TREND; TUPRS (1W: -2.95% | 1M: -6.12%) BELOW TREND.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Banking & Finance for continuation of leadership and earnings flow; Transportation for travel demand confirmation. Monitor Technology & Telecom for signs of mean reversion or deeper weakness.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Keep watch on XU100 momentum around the recent breakout zone (confirmation needed on sustained follow-through and weekly closes); catalysts include domestic macro prints, CBRT commentary, FX moves, oil and global risk sentiment. A failure to hold intra-week gains would favor tactical profit-taking.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
RISK-ON regime: low vol (24.4%) and 7/8 sectors positive, led by banks, transport and select energy names. Trade overweight cyclicals (AKBNK, YKBNK, TOASO, TRALT) while monitoring technology for downside risk and macro/currency triggers that could flip the tape quickly.
